codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly adds config hot-reload with token preservation for Codex (and Copilot) backends. The Registry now maintains a separate tokenStores map that survives LoadFromConfig calls, so in-memory tokens are not lost when backends are recreated. OAuth status interfaces are cleanly designed as optional backend interfaces. Web endpoints for OAuth login/callback/disconnect are properly implemented with correct error handling. The health endpoint correctly reflects auth status. 20 new tests provide thorough coverage of the hot-reload scenarios and OAuth flows.

issues.description: When a config hot-reload occurs while an OAuth PKCE flow is in progress (user has been redirected to the auth provider but hasn't completed the callback yet), the backend is recreated and the CodexOAuthHandler's in-memory 'pending' states map is discarded. The subsequent callback would fail with 'invalid or expired state parameter'. This is a narrow edge case since hot-reloads are operator-initiated (SIGHUP) and OAuth flows typically complete within seconds, but it's worth noting. No fix is needed now — the user would simply re-initiate login after reload.

The HTMX auto-refresh trigger (hx-trigger='every 30s') is placed on each individual backend status div, which means if there are N OAuth backends, there will be N parallel polling requests every 30 seconds. A single wrapper div with one hx-trigger would be more efficient, but this is a minor UX/performance concern, not a bug.

CopilotBackend.OAuthStatus() does not set NeedsReauth=true when there is no token, unlike CodexBackend which does. This means the health endpoint won't report 'degraded' status for an unauthenticated Copilot backend. This appears intentional (Copilot can auto-discover tokens from env vars/gh CLI), but the inconsistency between Codex and Copilot handling could be confusing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The OAuth endpoints use Chi URL parameters ({backend}) but AGENTS.md doesn't document this pattern for route registration. Other routes in the project use query parameters or form values. This Chi-specific URL param pattern is worth documenting for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: cmd/llmapiproxy/main.go:96-98 uses r.Get('/oauth/login/{backend}', ...) with chi.URLParam(r, 'backend') in internal/web/web.go:775

sharedStateObservations.observation: The library file codex-backend.md was already updated by this worker with comprehensive documentation of the token preservation pattern, OAuth status interfaces, web endpoints, and health endpoint changes. This is good — the worker proactively updated shared knowledge.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/codex-backend.md contains 'Config Hot-Reload (SIGHUP) with Token Preservation' section, 'OAuth Status Interfaces' section, and 'Web UI OAuth Endpoints' section

summary: PASS — The implementation is solid and well-tested. The core mechanism (tokenStores map preserved across LoadFromConfig calls) is clean and thread-safe under the existing RWMutex. The optional interface pattern (OAuthStatusProvider, OAuthLoginHandler, etc.) is well-designed and follows Go conventions. All 20 new tests pass reliably. The three non-blocking issues noted are edge cases (PKCE state loss during rare hot-reload-mid-OAuth, HTMX polling efficiency, and Copilot/Codex NeedsReauth inconsistency) that don't affect normal operation.
